title: PATH_RT_T: Path-length based Radiative Transfer model for Terrain
description: A Python implementation of a path-length based radiative transfer model for simulating vegetation reflectance over sloped terrain. The model accounts for 3D canopy structure using Canopy Height Models (CHM) and incorporates terrain effects on radiative transfer processes.
references: Li, W., Yan, G., Mu, X., Tong, Y., Zhou, K., & Xie, D. (2024). Modeling the hotspot effect for vegetation canopies based on path length distribution. Remote Sensing of Environment, 303, 113985. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rse.2023.113985
